description_long: This is a small, neoclassical bank building constructed primarily from light gray stone or concrete, featuring a prominent triangular pediment supported by two robust columns at the front. The roof is pitched and covered in a muted beige or gold material, with a matching trim that runs along the top edge of the structure. The main entrance is centrally located, framed by a rectangular doorway with a deep blue door and a decorative pediment above it. Both sides of the building have similar blue, rectangular windows, each topped with a small triangular pediment, echoing the main entrance's design. The rear of the building is plain, lacking windows or doors, and the structure sits on a raised platform with steps leading up to the entrance. The overall appearance is solid, formal, and symmetrical, evoking the traditional style of financial institutions.
description: A small, neoclassical bank with a gray stone exterior, gold-trimmed pitched roof, and a columned entrance with blue doors and windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with two columns, a blue door, and a triangular pediment.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a blue window with a pediment and part of the columned entrance.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wall with no windows or doors, and the gold-trimmed roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a blue window and pediment, plus a partial view of the entrance columns.
attribute_entrance_design: Single rectangular door, deep blue in color, framed by two stone columns and topped with a triangular pediment; no visible handles or glass panels.
